stepAnswer: Converting a sum to a product is useful when solving trigonometric equations that involve a sum of sines or cosines set to zero, as it allows factoring. For example, to solve $$\sin(5x)+\sin(3x)=0$$ we can convert it to $$2\sin(4x)\cos(x)=0$$ which is easier to solve.
stepTitle: Which of the following situations best describes when converting an equation from a sum to a product is useful, and provides a correct example?
answerLatex: Converting a sum to a product is useful when solving trigonometric equations that involve a sum of sines or cosines set to zero, as it allows factoring. For example, to solve $$\sin(5x)+\sin(3x)=0$$ we can convert it to $$2\sin(4x)\cos(x)=0$$ which is easier to solve.
choices: Converting a sum to a product is useful when solving trigonometric equations that involve a sum of sines or cosines set to zero, as it allows factoring. For example, to solve $$\sin(5x)+\sin(3x)=0$$ we can convert it to $$2\sin(4x)\cos(x)=0$$ which is easier to solve.
Converting a sum to a product is useful when simplifying expressions for integration. For example, to integrate $$\sin(5x)+\sin(3x)$$ we convert it to $$2\sin(4x)\cos(x)$$
Converting a sum to a product is useful when finding the amplitude of a function. For example, to find the amplitude of $$\sin(5x)+\sin(3x)$$ we convert it to $$2\sin(4x)\cos(x)$$
Converting a sum to a product is useful when verifying identities that involve products. For example, to verify $$\sin(5x)+\sin(3x)=2\sin(4x)\cos(x)$$ we convert the sum to a product.
